content.text: The system shall provide a Documents screen under the navigation path MHE → MMS → Documents.
the system to provide access to the Documents screen under MHE → MMS → Documents
I can manage and view all uploaded MHE-related documents.
the user opens the left navigation menu,
the user selects MHE → MMS → Documents,
the system shall navigate to the Documents screen.
When the user clicks MMS → Documents in the left navigation, the system shall open the Documents – Document Library page.
the system to open the Document Library page when I click MMS → Documents
I can view all stored documents.
the system shall open the Documents – Document Library page.

3.title: Display Page Title and Section Title

content.text: The system shall display a page title “Documents” and a section title “Document Library” at the top of the screen.
I can understand I am on the Documents screen.
the Documents Library page has loaded,
the system shall show “Documents” and “Document Library” at the top.

The system shall provide a Search box above the table that allows the user to filter the document list by typing any part of the Document Name, Entity Type, or Entity ID; results shall update based on the search text.
a search box to filter documents
I can quickly find required documents.
the user types in the Search box,
the text matches Document Name, Entity Type, or Entity ID,
the document list shall update in real time to show matching results.
The system shall provide an Entity filter dropdown (default value “All Entities”) that allows the user to filter the document list by specific entity types (e.g., MHE, Operator, SENSOR, etc.).
I can view documents linked to specific entities (MHE, Operator, SENSOR, etc.).
the user clicks the Entity dropdown,
a specific entity type is selected,
the document list shall refresh to show only documents for that entity.
The system shall provide an “Upload Document” button on the top-right of the Document Library section that, when clicked, opens the document upload flow (screen/popup) for adding a new document.
I can add new documents to the system.
the system shall open the document upload flow (screen/popup).
The system shall display the document list in a tabular format with the following column headers: Document Name, Entity Type, Entity ID, Upload Date, Action.
the system to display documents in a table
I can view document attributes clearly.
the system shall show columns: Document Name, Entity Type, Entity ID, Upload Date, Action.
Under Document Name, the system shall display an file/document icon followed by the saved document name.
file type and name are easily recognizable.
the system shall show a document/file icon followed by the document name.
Under Entity Type, the system shall display the associated entity type as a coloured label/badge (e.g., SENSOR (Purple colour), MHE (blue colour), OPERATOR (green colour).
entity types to appear as colored labels
the Entity Type column is rendered,
SENSOR shall appear Purple, MHE Blue, OPERATOR Green, etc.
Under Entity ID, the system shall display the unique ID of the entity to which the document is linked.
I know which entity the document belongs to.
the Entity ID shall be shown correctly in the respective column.
Under Upload Date, the system shall display the date and time on which the document was uploaded in the format “MMM DD, YYYY, HH:MM AM/PM”.
see when a document was uploaded
the Upload Date column is displayed,
the date/time must appear in “MMM DD, YYYY, HH:MM AM/PM” format.
Under Action, the system shall provide a vertical three-dot menu for each row; clicking this menu shall show context actions such as View/Download, and Delete
a contextual action menu for each document
I can perform actions such as view, download, or delete.
the user clicks the three-dot menu,
options View/Download and Delete shall appear.
The system shall support pagination at the bottom of the table, showing the current range (e.g., “1–5 of 5”) and navigation controls for first, previous, next, and last pages.
I can navigate through large lists of documents.
the number of documents exceeds the current page limit,
the system shall show page range (e.g., “1–5 of 5”) and navigation arrows (first, previous, next, last).
The system shall provide a “Rows per page” dropdown that allows the user to select how many records are displayed per page (e.g., 10, 25, 50).
control how many rows are displayed
I can adjust my viewing preference.
the rows per page dropdown is present,
the table shall refresh to show that many records.

15.title: Empty State When No Documents Found

content.text: If no documents are available for the selected filters, the system shall display an appropriate empty-state “+ No Documents found with subtitle message- upload you first document to get started”.  indicating that no documents were found.
a clear message when no documents match my filters
I understand that no results exist.
the applied filters produce zero results,
Upload your first document to get started.”
The document list shall be sorted by default in descending order of Upload Date (latest document shown at the top).
the latest documents to appear first
the list shall display newest uploads at the top.
Under Action, the system shall provide a vertical three-dot menu for each row; clicking this menu shall show context actions such as View/Download, and Delete
every document row supports View/Download and Delete.
any row in the table is rendered,
the user opens the action menu,
options View Document, Download, and Delete must be shown.
When the user clicks on the three-dots action menu in a document row, the system shall display the following options: View Document, Download, and Delete.
open actions for a specific document
the user clicks the three-dots on any row,
the correct options (View, Download, Delete) shall appear.
The system shall close the action menu automatically when the user clicks outside the menu or selects an option.
the system to close the action menu automatically
the UI remains clean and intuitive.
the user clicks outside or selects an option,
When the user clicks View Document, the system shall open a modal popup centred on the screen.
preview a document in a modal
I can view contents without downloading it.
a centered modal popup with the document preview shall open.
The modal shall display the document name, preview (if supported), and a close button (“X”).
the View Document modal to show the document name, preview, and a close button
I can quickly review the file and close the modal.
the user opens the View Document modal,
it shall show the document name, preview (if supported), and a close “X” button.
The background behind the modal shall be dimmed, and user interaction with the underlying screen shall be disabled until the modal is closed.
the background to dim when the modal is open
my focus is on the modal and accidental actions on the main screen are prevented.
the background must dim and the main page must become non-interactive.
When the user clicks Download, the system shall initiate a file download of the selected document.
the ability to download a document
I can save it locally for viewing.
the user selects Download from the action menu,
the document file shall begin downloading.
After download begins, the system shall display a toast notification at the top-right corner of the screen with the message: “Download started”.
a toast message when the download starts
I know the download has initiated.
the system shall show a toast “Download started” at the top-right corner.
When the user clicks Delete, the system shall open a confirmation popup asking the user to confirm or cancel the delete action.
a confirmation popup before deleting a document
I do not accidentally delete important files.
the user clicks Delete on any document row,
a confirmation popup shall appear asking user to confirm or cancel.

The confirmation popup shall include at least: message text- “Delete documents”. The popup shall display the message: “Are you sure you want to delete (MHE, OPERATOR, SENSOR Document)”? This action cannot be undone.”, a Cancel button, and a Delete button (in red).
the delete confirmation popup to include a clear warning
I understand the consequences of deleting a document.
the Delete confirmation popup is displayed,
Text: “Are you sure you want to delete (MHE, OPERATOR, SENSOR Document)? This action cannot be undone.”
The system shall delete the selected document only if the user clicks Delete in the confirmation popup.
the system to delete a document only when I confirm
the system shall permanently delete the selected document.
Upon successful deletion, the system shall remove the document from the list and refresh the table view.
the document table to refresh after deletion
the removed file no longer appears in the list.
the deleted document shall be removed from the list.
The system shall display a success toast notification at the top-right corner of the screen stating: “Document deleted successfully”.
a confirmation toast after deleting a document
I know the operation was successful.
a toast “Document deleted successfully” shall appear at the top-right.
When the user clicks Upload Document on the Document Library page, the system shall display the Upload Document modal centred on the screen.
the Upload Document modal to open when I click Upload Document
I can upload a new file.
it shall open the Upload Document modal centered on screen.
The modal shall dim the background and disable interaction with the underlying screen until the modal is closed.
the background dimmed when the upload modal is open
I stay focused and avoid interacting with the underlying page.
the Upload Document modal is open,
user interaction with the main screen must be disabled.
The modal shall display the title “Upload Document” and a short description text – “Upload documents for MHE, Operator, or Sensor. Add an optional description for the document”. guiding the user.
the upload modal to show title and description
I understand how to upload documents correctly.
Description: “Upload documents for MHE, Operator, or Sensor. Add an optional description for the document.”
The modal shall contain an Entity Type dropdown, that includes options MHE, OPERATOR, SENSOR which is a mandatory field marked with a red asterisk (*).
the system knows what entity the document belongs to.
the Upload Document modal is open,
the Entity Type dropdown is displayed,
it must show options: MHE, OPERATOR, SENSOR and be marked mandatory (*).

34.title: Entity ID Dropdown Enabled After Entity Type

content.text: The Entity ID dropdown shall be mandatory filed and remain disabled until the user selects an Entity Type.
Entity ID to unlock only after selecting Entity Type
I only see valid IDs for the chosen entity.
Entity ID dropdown shall be disabled.
the user selects an Entity Type,

35.title: Document Type Based on Entity ID

content.text: The Document Type dropdown shall be mandatory field and shall display options as per selected Entity Type and shall remain disabled until the user selects an Entity ID.
Document Type to depend on selected Entity ID
Document Type dropdown shall remain disabled.
it shall show valid document type options.
The modal shall contain a mandatory File Name input field where the user must enter the document name.
the File Name field is displayed,
the system shall prevent form submission.
The modal shall contain a file upload field (scroll down area) enabling the user to select and upload a file.
I can attach the document file.
the Upload Document modal is open,
the user scrolls to the file upload section,
they shall see a file upload input area.
The system shall provide a Description text box allowing the user to enter an optional description for the document.
I can include context for the document.
the description text box is displayed,
the description shall accept multi-line text.
The Description field shall support multi-line text entry with a character limit (system-defined, e.g., 250–500 characters).
I do not enter excessively long notes.
the user types in the description box,
the text reaches the character limit (250–500),
no additional characters shall be accepted.
The system shall display a Document upload section, which is a mandatory field marked with a red asterisk (*).
the document upload field to be mandatory
the user tries to submit the form,
the system shall block submission and show a mandatory field error.
The Document upload section shall support both click-to-upload or drag-and-drop functionality.
the document upload section to support click-to-upload and drag-and-drop
I can upload files conveniently using my preferred method.
the user is in the Upload Document modal,
they click the upload area or drag a file into it,
the system shall accept the file using either method.
The system shall allow uploading files of types PDF, DOC, and Images (PNG, JPG, JPEG).
the system to accept only PDF, DOC, PNG, JPG, and JPEG files
only supported file formats are uploaded.
the file format is PDF, DOC, PNG, JPG, or JPEG,
the system shall allow the upload.
The system shall restrict the maximum file size to 10 MB per uploaded document.
the system to restrict file size to 10 MB
uploads remain efficient and within allowed limits.
the system shall block the upload and show an error.
If the user attempts to upload a file exceeding 10 MB or with an unsupported format, the system shall display an inline error message and prevent uploading.
see an inline error when uploading a too-large or unsupported file
I can correct the issue immediately.
the user attempts to upload an invalid file,
the file is too large or of unsupported type,
the system shall show an inline error and prevent uploading.
After selecting a valid file, the system shall show a preview or file name with an option to remove or replace the file.
the system to show a file preview or name after selecting a valid file
I can confirm the correct file was chosen.
the system shall display the file name or thumbnail and an option to remove/replace.
The system shall not allow the user to proceed with upload if the Document field is empty and shall show an inline validation message.
display message - Please fill all required fields with red colour.
the system to prevent document upload when no file is selected
the system shall show a red inline message: “Please fill all required fields.”
The modal shall include two buttons at the bottom: Cancel (left) and Upload in Blue colour (right).
a Cancel and Upload (blue) button in the modal
I can either dismiss or submit my upload.
the Upload Document modal is open,
the buttons Cancel (left) and Upload (right, blue) shall be displayed.
Clicking Cancel shall close the modal without saving or uploading any information.
Cancel to close the modal without saving
the modal shall close with no data saved.
Clicking Upload shall validate all mandatory fields; if any required field is missing, an inline validation error shall be displayed.
the system to validate required fields before uploading
the system shall show an inline validation error and block upload.
If all validations pass, the system shall upload the document, close the modal, refresh the document list, and display a success toast in the top-right corner.- “Document uploaded successfully”.
the system to upload the document and refresh the list
I can see the newly added document.
the document shall be uploaded, the modal shall close, the list shall refresh, and a toast “Document uploaded successfully” shall appear.
The modal shall include a close (“X”) icon in the top-right corner, allowing the user to close the modal.
close the upload modal using the (X) icon
the modal shall close without saving.
When the user clicks on the All-Entities dropdown, the system shall display the following filter options: MHE, Operator, and Sensor, along with All Entities as the default option.
the All-Entities dropdown to show MHE, Operator, Sensor
I can filter documents by entity type.
the user clicks the All-Entities dropdown,
it shall show: All Entities, MHE, Operator, Sensor.

53.title: Filter Document List by Entity Type

content.text: The system shall allow the user to select any one of the entity types (MHE, Operator, Sensor). On selection, the document list shall immediately refresh to show only documents belonging to the selected entity type
the list to refresh when I select an entity type
the user selects an entity type,
the grid shall refresh showing only documents belonging to that entity type.

When MHE is selected, the system shall display the MHE Document List screen containing: the table with columns (Document Name, Entity Type, Entity ID, Upload Date, Action), the Upload Document button, and the Select MHE dropdown.
the system to show the MHE Document List when MHE is selected
MHE is selected in entity type,
it shall display the MHE table, Upload Document button, and Select MHE dropdown.
The Select MHE dropdown shall display all MHE entity IDs available in the system. The user shall be able to filter the list using a search bar inside the dropdown.
the Select MHE dropdown to list all MHE IDs with search
I can quickly find a specific MHE.
the Select MHE dropdown is opened,
the user types in the search bar,
the dropdown shall filter available MHE IDs.

56.title: Filter MHE Documents by All MHE

content.text: When the user clicks “All MHE” from the select dropdown, the system shall filter the document list to show Documents of All MHEs.
the table shall show documents for all MHEs.

57.title: Filter MHE Documents by Selected MHE

content.text: When the user selects a specific MHE from the Select MHE dropdown, the system shall filter the document list and show only the documents associated with that selected MHE.
filter the list by a chosen MHE
only documents for that specific MHE display.
the user selects a specific MHE,
only documents belonging to that MHE shall appear.

58.title: Empty State for No MHE Documents

content.text: If no MHE documents are available, the system shall display an empty state message: “No entities available”
see an empty-state message when no MHE documents exist
I know no files are associated.
When Operator is selected from the All-Entities dropdown, the system shall display the Operator Document List screen with the standard columns and Select Operator dropdown.
the system to show Operator documents
Operator is selected in entity filter,
it shall show standard column table + Select Operator dropdown.

60.title: Populate Select Operator Dropdown with Search

content.text: The Select Operator dropdown shall list all available operators (with search functionality).
the Select Operator dropdown to list all operators with search
the Select Operator dropdown is opened,
the user uses the search bar,
the dropdown shall filter operators accordingly.
When the user selects a specific operator, the document list shall refresh and display only the documents uploaded for that operator.
the document list to refresh when I select a specific operator
I can view only the documents uploaded for that operator.
the user selects a specific operator from the Select Operator dropdown,
the system shall refresh and show only documents related to that operator.

62.title: Show Empty State When No Operator Data Exists

content.text: If no operator entities exist or no documents exist, an appropriate message shall be displayed such as “No entities available” or “No Documents Found”.
an informative message when operators or operator documents do not exist
I know there is no data available.
there are no operators or no operator-specific documents,
When Sensor is selected, the system shall display the Sensor Document List screen with the standard table columns and Select Sensor dropdown.
the system to show the Sensor Document List screen when Sensor is selected
Sensor is selected in the All-Entities dropdown,
the system shall display the standard table columns and Select Sensor dropdown.
The Select Sensor dropdown shall list all sensors associated with the system (with search functionality).
the Select Sensor dropdown to list all sensors with search functionality
I can quickly find a specific sensor.
the user opens the Select Sensor dropdown,
the system shall list all sensors and support search filtering.

65.title: Empty State When No Sensors Exist

content.text: If no sensors exist, the dropdown shall display “No entities available” and the document list shall remain empty.
the dropdown to indicate when sensors are not available
I understand why the table is empty.
no sensors exist in the system,
the user opens the Select Sensor dropdown,
the document list shall remain empty.
When a sensor is selected, the system shall refresh the list to display only documents associated with that sensor.
the document list to refresh when I select a sensor
I only see documents linked to that sensor.
the user selects a specific sensor,
only documents for that selected sensor shall appear.
All filtered document lists (MHE / Operator / Sensor) shall retain the Upload Document button and the three-dot Action menu (View, Download, Delete) for each document row.
Upload Document and the Action (⋮) menu to always be available
I can manage documents even when filtered.
a filtered document list (MHE / Operator / Sensor) is displayed,
the Upload Document button and three-dot Action menu (View, Download, Delete) shall appear for each row.

68.title: Provide Search Bar Across All Entity Screens

content.text: The system shall display a search bar in all filtered lists to search documents by name, entity type, or entity ID
the search bar to be visible for all filtered lists
I can search documents by name, entity type, or entity ID.
any filtered entity view is open,
the user enters text in the search bar,
the list shall filter results in real time across name, entity type, and entity ID.

69.title: Consistent Pagination & Sorting on All Screens

content.text: Pagination, rows-per-page control, and sorting by Upload Date shall work consistently across all entity-specific screens
pagination, rows-per-page, and sorting to work consistently
I can navigate documents regardless of filter type.
any filtered document list is displayed,
the user interacts with pagination, rows per page, or sorting,
the behavior shall remain consistent and sort by Upload Date by default.
When the user clicks Clear All Filters, the system shall reset all applied filters including Entity Type and Entity ID.
the Clear All Filters control to reset all filter selections
I can return to the unfiltered view easily.
the user clicks Clear All Filters,
all filters including Entity Type and Entity ID shall reset.

71.title: Return to All-Entities Screen After Clear

content.text: After clearing filters, the system shall return the user to the All-Entities screen.
the system to return me to the All-Entities document list after clearing filters
I can see all uploaded documents again.
the system shall show the All-Entities screen.

72.title: Display All Documents on All-Entities Screen

content.text: The All-Entities screen shall display all documents across MHE, Operator, and Sensor without any filtering applied.
the All-Entities screen to show documents from all entity types
the user is on the All-Entities screen,
the system shall display documents from MHE, Operator, and Sensor.
